items.title: Examine preventing CPD hire of officers from ICE / HSI / CBP hired on or after Jan 20, 2025
items.summary: The Council asked the City Manager to examine whether and how Cambridge can prevent the Police Department from hiring any officer hired by ICE, Homeland Security Investigations, or Customs and Border Patrol on or after January 20, 2025, and report back. Adopted as amended unanimously.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to direct the relevant City staff and departments to examine if and how the City may prevent the hiring by the Cambridge Police Department of any sworn officer who was hired on or after January 20, 2025 by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Homeland Security Investigations, or Customs and Border Patrol, and report back to the City Council in a timely manner. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [9-0-0]

rationale.doc.sponsor:al_zubi: COUNCILLOR AL-ZUBI listed as sponsor in Final Actions

items.title: Examine permitting third-party guarantors / co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants
items.summary: The Council asked the Community Development Department to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants, and recommend whether such a policy could responsibly expand access. Adopted 8-0-1, with Councillor Zusy voting Present.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to direct the Community Development Department (CDD) to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified thirdparty guarantors or co-signers for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units and provide recommendations as to whether such a policy could responsibly expand access to these units. RESULT: Adopted [8-0-1-0]

rationale.doc.sponsor:azeem: VICE MAYOR AZEEM listed as sponsor
rationale.housing.tenure:rent: Inclusionary Housing units are typically rental
rationale.civic.councillor_vote:azeem:yea: YEAS list includes VICE MAYOR AZEEM

items.title: Draft ordinance restricting use of City property and personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement
items.summary: The Council asked the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement, and to evaluate whether the protections should be added to the Welcoming Community Ordinance. Adopted as amended unanimously.
items.body: That the City Manager is hereby requested to direct the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for the purposes of federal civil immigration enforcement, and that the Law Department evaluate whether these protections should be adopted as an amendment to the Welcoming Community Ordinance. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [9-0-0]

items.title: Raise residential parking permit fee to $75, $25 subsidy for low-income; remove senior exemption; cap at 2 cars/household
items.summary: A policy order proposing major changes to the residential parking permit program: a $75 base fee, a subsidized $25 fee for residents in affordable housing or SNAP, removal of the existing senior exemption, and a cap of 2 permits per household (down from 4). Charter Right exercised by Councillor Simmons; no final action at this meeting.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to raise the fee of the parking permit program for all residents to $75, consider how to include a self-identified check off option so as not to increase administrative costs for a subsidized fee of $25 for residents who live in affordable housing, are enrolled in a program such as SNAP or are low income, remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program and lower the number of cars that individual residents are allowed to get a residential parking permit for from four to two. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026. RESULT: Charter Right

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons

rationale.doc.status:charter_right_exercised: CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS

items.title: Plan to expand free early child care offerings, including means-tested models and outside funding
items.summary: The Council asked the City Manager to work with the Council and relevant departments on expanding free early child care offerings, including means-tested programs and exploration of state and foundation funding. Charter Right exercised by Councillor Simmons; no final action at this meeting.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which Cambridge can expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming, and exploration of non-City funding sources, from the state or foundations. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026. RESULT: Charter Right

items.title: Plan with MIT and CASPAR to renovate and expand 240 Albany Street shelter, with low-threshold support
items.summary: The Council asked the City Manager to begin discussions with MIT, CASPAR, the Human Services and Veterans Committee co-chairs, relevant departments, and shelter residents to develop a plan to renovate and expand 240 Albany Street, providing additional low-threshold shelter, housing, and daytime services and accommodating residents impacted by construction. Adopted unanimously.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to begin discussions with MIT, CASPAR, the co-chairs of the Human Services and Veterans Committee, all relevant city departments and the shelter residents to develop a plan to renovate and expand 240 Albany Street in order to provide additional low threshold shelter, housing and day time services, while accommodating shelter residents impacted by the construction closure with housing and support, including low threshold options. RESULT: Adopted [9-0-0]

items.title: Calendar: review digital equity work and internet access programs — Adopted as Amended
items.summary: A previously charter-righted policy order asking the City Manager to review the city's digital equity work and existing internet access programs, assess whether residents' digital needs are being met, and report back by June 2026. Adopted as amended unanimously at this meeting.
items.body: That the City Manager ask the appropriate departments to review the city's digital equity work to date, including the study's recommendations and all steps taken since the study conclusion in order to update the evaluation of existing internet access programs, assessing whether residents' digital needs are or could be better provided for and to propose how we can better meet their needs and to report back to the Council by June 2026 on internet access programs. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR AL-ZUBI IN COUNCIL JANUARY 26, 2026. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [9-0-0]

items.body: Condolences to the Family of Janet Doucette. COUNCILLOR SIMMONS, COUNCILLOR AL-ZUB, VICE MAYOR AZEEM, COUNCILLOR FLAHERTY, COUNCILLOR MCGOVERN, COUNCILLOR NOLAN, COUNCILLOR SOBRINHO-WHEELER, COUNCILLOR ZUSY, MAYOR SIDDIQUII. RESULT: Adopted [9-0-0]

rationale.doc.kind:RES: Section X. RESOLUTIONS / late agenda item

items.title: Calendar: prepare new home rule petition for a Real Estate Transfer Fee — Tabled as Amended
items.summary: A previously charter-righted communication from the City Manager on Awaiting Report 25-69, requesting a review of the prior home rule petition and preparation of a new petition that would allow Cambridge to enact a Real Estate Transfer Fee. At this meeting the item was Tabled as Amended 6-3-0, with Councillors Al-Zubi, Nolan, and Sobrinho-Wheeler voting nay.
items.body: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-69, regarding a review of the previous home rule petition and prepare a new petition that would allow Cambridge to enact a Real Estate Transfer Fee to be sent to the state legislature. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR NOLAN IN COUNCIL JANUARY 26, 2026 TABLED AS AMENDED IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026. RESULT: Tabled as Amended [6-3-0]

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Nolan

rationale.doc.kind:AR: originated as Awaiting Report 25-69 / now AR-2026-06
rationale.doc.item_id:ar-2026-06: Awaiting Report 25-69 corresponds to AR 2026-06 in the AR list
rationale.doc.stage:tabled: TABLED AS AMENDED IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026
rationale.doc.stage:carryover: Charter Right exercised at prior meeting; reappears on Calendar
rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: real estate transfer fee directly affects housing transactions
rationale.housing.tenure:any: transfer fee affects owners and indirectly market for renters

rationale.civic.councillor_vote:azeem:yea: YEAS list includes VICE MAYOR AZEEM

items.title: Open city-owned parking lots, including Tobin/Darby Vassal, for after-hours residential use
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to work with the School Department, DPW, and other departments to make city-owned parking lots — especially school complexes including the Tobin/Darby Vassal under-construction parking — available for after-hours use by residents. Status pending (no Council action recorded).
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to ensure that all city owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes, including the still under construction parking at Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, could be made available for after-hours use by residents. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-29. Councillor Nolan

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.transport.subject:parking: city owned parking lots...available for after-hours use

items.title: Establish formal policy on City role and finances for large public events by Cambridge nonprofits
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to consult with City departments, including the City Solicitor's Office, to establish a formal policy defining the City's role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based non-profit and not-for-profit organizations. Status pending.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to consult with the appropriate City departments, including the City Solicitor's Office, to establish a formal policy that clearly defines the City's role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based non-profit and not-for-profit organizations. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-48. Councillor Simmons, Councillor McGovern

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.topic.area:arts: large-scale public events typically include cultural / community events

items.title: Outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators for additional Ordinance Committee recommendations
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to seek input from the community and direct outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators, and to develop additional recommendations based on Ordinance Committee discussion. Status pending.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to work with all relevant departments to seek input from the community as well as direct outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators and develop additional recommendations based on the discussion in the Ordinance Committee. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-51. Councillor Nolan, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor McGovern, Mayor Siddiqui

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: short-term rental and condo regulation is housing/zoning policy

items.title: Evaluate and install speed humps along Fayerweather Street between Walnut and Field
items.summary: Awaiting Report directing City departments, including the Department of Transportation, to evaluate and install speed humps along Fayerweather Street, with particular attention to the stretch between Walnut Street and Field Street. Status pending.
items.body: Direct appropriate City departments, including the Department of Transportation, to evaluate and install a series of speed humps along Fayerweather Street, with particular attention to the stretch between Walnut Street and Field Street. Original Awaiting Report #31840. Councillor Simmons, Councillor Flaherty, Councillor McGovern

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST

rationale.topic.area:public_safety: traffic safety on a residential corridor

items.title: Review City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District oversight, including visitor kiosk
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Solicitor and CDD to report on (i) legal parameters for City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions, and (ii) oversight and accountability of the visitor information kiosk. Status pending.
items.body: Direct the City Solicitor and the Community Development Department to review and report back to the Council on (i) legal parameters related to City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions, and (ii) oversight and accountability related to the operation of the visitor information kiosk, and related governance expectations, including alignment with City priorities. Original Awaiting Report ID #31841. Councillor Simmons, Councillor Flaherty

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.topic.area:small_business: Tourism Destination Marketing affects local merchants and small businesses

items.summary: Awaiting Report asking departments to prepare for a Council discussion on best future uses for City-owned properties and the processes for redevelopment. Status pending.
items.body: Work with relevant departments to prepare for a Council discussion on best future uses for City-owned properties and the processes for redevelopment. Original Awaiting Report #31842. Councillor Nolan, Mayor Siddiqui, Councillor Zusy, Councillor Al-Zubi

items.title: Incorporate plant-based solutions into Sustainable Cambridge initiative and City/School purchasing
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to incorporate plant-based food solutions into the Sustainable Cambridge initiative and into purchasing for City- and School-related events. Status pending.
items.body: That the City Manager work with relevant departments to incorporate plant-based solutions into the Sustainable Cambridge initiative and purchasing practices in City-operated and School-related events. Councillor Nolan, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor McGovern, Mayor Siddiqui

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.topic.area:climate: plant-based purchasing as part of Sustainable Cambridge

items.title: Pilot pedestrianization projects in 2026 without consultant; consider Brattle/JFK Square redesign
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to prioritize pedestrianization pilot projects in 2026 without an extensive consultant study, and in the medium term consider how additional pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street, by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street, could enhance Harvard Square. Status pending.
items.body: That the City Manager work with relevant City departments to prioritize pilot projects in 2026 without the use of a consultant for an extensive pedestrianization study, and in the medium-term consider how additional pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street could enhance the Square. Councillor Nolan, Councillor Simmons, Mayor Siddiqui, Councillor Zusy

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.transport.subject:pedestrianization: pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street
rationale.transport.mode:drive: restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street affects drivers
rationale.geo.neighborhood:mid_cambridge: Brattle/JFK area is in/adjacent to Mid Cambridge

items.title: Update on Transition Wellness Center voucher program implementation and outcomes
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager for an update on the housing voucher program serving former Transition Wellness Center residents, including issuance status, prioritization criteria, staffing, lease-up support, voucher discrimination, and ongoing evaluation. Status pending.
items.body: That the City Manager provide an update to the council, including (but not limited to): current implementation status, including vouchers issued to former residents of the Transition Wellness Center; final prioritization criteria adopted; staffing structure and support services; strategies employed to address voucher discrimination and support successful lease-up; program parameters; challenges encountered during implementation; and plans for ongoing program assessment and evaluation. Councillor Al-Zubi, Councillor McGovern

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.housing.tenure:rent: vouchers used by tenants in rentals
rationale.housing.affordability:public_housing: voucher program serving former TWC residents
rationale.topic.area:public_health: former Transition Wellness Center residents (recovery context)

items.title: Comprehensive review of safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to conduct a comprehensive review of safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, including physical access controls, visitor screening, on-site security, emergency response and evacuation, and surveillance/alarm technologies. Status pending.
items.body: That the City Manager conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, including an assessment of physical access controls, visitor screening practices, on-site security staffing and training, emergency response and evacuation protocols, and the use of security technologies such as surveillance systems and alarm monitoring. Councillor Flaherty, Councillor Simmons

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIII. APPENDED INFORMATION — AWAITING REPORT LIST
rationale.geo.neighborhood:mid_cambridge: City Hall is in Mid Cambridge / Central Square area
rationale.topic.area:public_safety: comprehensive review of...safety and security measures
